Question: ⓐ Graph $$f ( x ) = x^{2} , g ( x ) = x^{2} + 1 ,$$ and $$h ( x ) = x^{2} - 1$$ on the same rectangular coordinate system. ⓑ Describe what effect adding a constant to the function has on the basic parabola.
Answer: ⓐ ⓑ The graph of $$g ( x ) = x^{2} + 1$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted up 1 unit. The graph of $$h ( x ) = x^{2} - 1$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted down 1 unit.

Question: ⓐ Graph $$f ( x ) = x^{2} , g ( x ) = x^{2} + 6 ,$$ and $$h ( x ) = x^{2} - 6$$ on the same rectangular coordinate system. ⓑ Describe what effect adding a constant to the function has on the basic parabola.
Answer: ⓐ ⓑ The graph of $$h ( x ) = x^{2} + 6$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted up 6 units. The graph of $$h ( x ) = x^{2} - 6$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted down 6 units.

Question: Graph $$f ( x ) = x^{2} - 5$$ using a vertical shift.
Graph $$f ( x ) = x^{2} + 7$$ using a vertical shift.

ⓐ Graph $$f ( x ) = x^{2} , g ( x ) = ( x + 2 )^{2} ,$$ and $$h ( x ) = ( x - 2 )^{2}$$ on the same rectangular coordinate system. ⓑ Describe what effect adding a constant to the function has on the basic parabola.
Answer: ⓐ ⓑ The graph of $$g ( x ) = ( x + 2 )^{2}$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted left 2 units. The graph of $$h ( x ) = ( x - 2 )^{2}$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shift right 2 units.

Question: ⓐ Graph $$f ( x ) = x^{2} , g ( x ) = x^{2} + 5 ,$$ and $$h ( x ) = x^{2} - 5$$ on the same rectangular coordinate system. ⓑ Describe what effect adding a constant to the function has on the basic parabola.
Answer: ⓐ ⓑ The graph of $$g ( x ) = ( x + 5 )^{2}$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted left 5 units. The graph of $$h ( x ) = ( x - 5 )^{2}$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted right 5 units.
